$comment: Fleet CLI verb and flag contract for admin tool integration. Defines the grammar that LibreSpot.Cli.exe exposes for Intune, PDQ, SCCM, WinRM, and PSRemoting use cases. Exit codes reference fleet-exit-codes.json; elevation requirements reference elevation-boundary.json.

verbs.description: Install or reinstall LibreSpot with SpotX + Spicetify.
verbs.notes: Primary install workflow. JSON output is not supported because install emits a stream of progress events via NDJSON. Use 'plan' for a JSON-only preview.

verbs.description: Reapply SpotX and Spicetify patches after a Spotify update.
verbs.notes: Refreshes patches and restores saved Spicetify state. Typically triggered by the watcher after detecting a Spotify update.

verbs.description: Check whether LibreSpot is installed and compliant with the desired state.
verbs.notes: Returns exit code 0 for compliant, 10 for not installed, 11 for drift, 12 for repair needed. The --intune flag formats output for Intune Win32 detection scripts.

verbs.description: Full health report covering Spotify, SpotX, Spicetify, watcher, and LibreSpot state.
verbs.notes: Emits a structured health report. Designed for dashboards, monitoring, and pre-install triage. Does not mutate any state.

verbs.description: Validate an answer file against the JSON schema and emit diagnostics.
verbs.notes: Exit 0 means the answer file is valid; exit 2 means validation errors were found. Includes typo suggestions for misspelled keys.

verbs.description: Show what install/reapply would do without executing any changes.
verbs.notes: Dry-run by nature — never mutates. Produces an operation plan in JSON that describes each step, required downloads, and expected changes. Use this verb when a single JSON plan document is preferred over install --dry-run --ndjson streaming output.

verbs.description: Run a specific repair action identified by its repair ID from a health report.
verbs.notes: Repair IDs come from the 'status' health report. Each repair action targets a single issue (e.g., stale watcher, missing Marketplace, version mismatch).

verbs.description: Register the auto-reapply scheduled task that patches Spotify after updates.
verbs.notes: Registers a user-level scheduled task (no admin required). The task runs at logon and watches for Spotify updates.
Removes the scheduled task registered by 'watcher install'. No-op if the task does not exist (exits 0).

verbs.description: Uninstall LibreSpot customizations, optionally removing Spicetify and Spotify.
verbs.notes: Default behavior removes LibreSpot and Spicetify customizations but keeps Spotify intact. --purge also removes LibreSpot config, logs, and backups. --keep-spotify is the default and is accepted for explicitness.

verbs.description: Export a redacted support bundle containing logs, config, and diagnostics.
verbs.notes: Produces a ZIP bundle with redacted logs, sanitized config, health report snapshot, and system info. The --output flag specifies the destination path; defaults to the current directory.

verbs.description: Show LibreSpot version, build metadata, and dependency versions.
verbs.notes: Exits 0 unconditionally. JSON output includes LibreSpot version, assembly version, runtime info, SpotX pin, Spicetify pin, Marketplace pin, and theme pin.

globalFlags.description: Emit machine-readable JSON output to stdout.
globalFlags.notes: Only available on verbs with supportsJson=true. Mutually exclusive with --ndjson.

globalFlags.description: Emit newline-delimited JSON progress events to stdout.
globalFlags.notes: Only available on verbs with supportsNdjson=true. Each line is a self-contained JSON object. Designed for real-time streaming consumption.

globalFlags.description: Suppress all interactive prompts and non-essential output.
globalFlags.notes: Implies --yes for confirmation prompts. Required for unattended deployment via Intune, SCCM, and PDQ.

globalFlags.description: Reduce output verbosity; only errors and warnings are emitted.
globalFlags.notes: Less aggressive than --silent. Prompts still appear unless --yes is also specified.

globalFlags.description: Path to a JSON answer file that supplies configuration values.
globalFlags.notes: Answer file schema is defined in librespot-answer.schema.json. Validated before any mutation occurs.

globalFlags.description: Named configuration profile to apply from the answer file.
globalFlags.notes: Selects a named profile section within the answer file. Ignored if --answer-file is not specified.

globalFlags.description: Override the default LibreSpot configuration directory.
globalFlags.notes: Defaults to %LOCALAPPDATA%\LibreSpot for user scope, %ProgramData%\LibreSpot for machine scope.

globalFlags.description: Override the default log output directory.
globalFlags.notes: Defaults to %ProgramData%\LibreSpot\logs for machine-scope operations, %LOCALAPPDATA%\LibreSpot\logs for user-scope.
Determines whether operations target the per-user or per-machine Spotify installation. Defaults to 'user'.

globalFlags.description: Accept the end-user license agreement non-interactively.
globalFlags.notes: Required for silent/unattended installs. Without this flag in silent mode, the CLI exits with code 2.

globalFlags.description: Suppress automatic Spotify restart after a mutating operation.
globalFlags.notes: By default, install/reapply/repair restart Spotify so the user sees changes immediately. Fleet deployments typically pass this flag.

globalFlags.description: Simulate the operation without making any changes.
globalFlags.notes: Only available on verbs with supportsDryRun=true. Produces the same output structure as a real run but skips all mutations. Exit code reflects what would have happened.
Implied by --silent. Useful when running interactively but wanting to skip confirmation prompts.

globalFlags.description: Attach a caller-supplied correlation ID to all log entries and output.
globalFlags.notes: Passed through to NDJSON events, health reports, and receipts. Useful for correlating LibreSpot operations with deployment system job IDs.

verbSpecificFlags.description: Format detect output for Intune Win32 app detection scripts.
verbSpecificFlags.notes: Writes a single-line stdout message that Intune detection scripts can match. Combines with --json for structured Intune-compatible output.

verbSpecificFlags.description: Identifier of the specific repair action to execute.
verbSpecificFlags.notes: Repair IDs are surfaced by the 'status' verb in the health report's repairActions array.

verbSpecificFlags.description: Remove LibreSpot configuration, logs, backups, and cached data in addition to the customizations.
verbSpecificFlags.notes: Without --purge, uninstall leaves config and logs intact for forensics or reinstall.

verbSpecificFlags.description: Keep Spotify installed when uninstalling LibreSpot customizations.
verbSpecificFlags.notes: This is the default behavior. The flag exists for explicitness in deployment scripts.

verbSpecificFlags.description: Destination file path for the exported support bundle.
verbSpecificFlags.notes: Defaults to .\LibreSpot-support-<timestamp>.zip in the current directory.

parserBehavior.slashPrefixAliases: Only /S is recognized as a slash-prefixed alias (Windows convention). All other flags use -- or - prefixes.
parserBehavior.notes: Unknown flags cause immediate exit with code 2 and a suggestion of the closest known flag. This happens before any mutation, so partially-applied changes are impossible from typos.

designPrinciples: Non-mutating verbs (detect, status, validate, plan, version) never require admin elevation.
Mutating verbs fail with exit code 50 if admin elevation is missing, before any changes are made.
Every verb produces deterministic exit codes documented in fleet-exit-codes.json.
--dry-run on mutating verbs produces the same output structure as a real run but skips all filesystem/registry writes.
NDJSON is the streaming format for long-running mutating operations; JSON is the snapshot format for read-only queries.
Flag conflicts (e.g., --json + --ndjson) are caught at parse time and exit with code 2.
The CLI shares backend code with the WPF desktop shell — verbs map 1:1 to backend actions in elevation-boundary.json.
